specific_concerns: The Sanskrit quotation 'kutas tvā kaśmalam idam viṣame samupasthitam' uttered inside an IC's Zoom rebuke is a stylized device — a serious practitioner might read this as putting Sanskrit in modern mouths a bit theatrically. But the layer keeps the quotation as a structural marker, not as a fashion accessory.
The triad 'anārya / asvargyam / akīrti-karam' is reduced to 'not the customer, not the team, not your own record' — losing the soteriological asvargyam. That is scope-restriction, not trivialization.
verdict_reason: The verse honors the source by preserving the sharp-diagnostic-question form, by quoting the Sanskrit verbatim, by naming the moment as viṣama-kāla, and by explicitly disclaiming use as a license-to-be-harsh-with-juniors. A serious practitioner of Hindu philosophy would find the verse used carefully and not flippantly. The Krishna-Arjuna senior-to-senior register is preserved as a structural feature, not collapsed into a generic 'leadership tip.'
